name: UN54
notes: ORWO Universal Negative Film UN 54. Panchromatic medium-speed B&W negative camera film; can be reversal-processed (ORWO instruction 4185) for direct positives. Grey triacetate safety base 135 um. RMS granularity 12 (net diffuse density 1.0, 48 um aperture). MTF factor m30 = 0.80. Negative processing per ORWO instruction 1182 (D96) to avg gradient 0.65. Also sold in 135/36exp as Wolfen UN54 (https://www.orwo.shop/en-us/products/wolfen-un54). Manufacturer: Filmotec GmbH, Wolfen.

name: NC500
notes: Wolfen NC500 color negative film. Cinema negative stock without remjet backing, so develops in standard C-41; ECN2 gives a flatter gradation. 125 um grey triacetate base. ISO 400/27. Daylight-balanced; densitometry status M. Coated by InovisCoat GmbH. Product page: https://www.orwo.shop/en-us/products/orwo-nc500-colour-cine-35mm

name: NC400
notes: Wolfen NC400 color negative film. Kodak C-41 process. 125 um grey triacetate base. ISO 400/27. Daylight-balanced; densitometry status M. Coated by InovisCoat GmbH. Product pages: https://www.orwo.shop/en-us/products/wolfen-nc400-36exp and https://www.orwo.shop/en-us/products/orwo-16mm-nc400-colour-cine

name: P30
notes: FERRANIA P30. 80 ISO panchromatic B&W motion-picture-heritage film for still photography, high silver content, ultrafine grain. Shoot at box speed 80 ISO; not DX coded. Traditional B&W chemistry (best-practices PDF lists D-76, D-96, HC-110, Rodinal, XTOL). Best Practices PDF: https://www.filmferrania.com/s/P30_Best_Practices_v3.pdf . Base not stated on official sources; omitted. Sourced from filmferrania.com (the .it domain has lapsed and now serves unrelated content).

name: Orto
notes: Ferrania Orto. 50 ISO orthochromatic B&W film, high silver content, practically imperceptible grain. New product developed with modern chemicals for a classic orthochromatic (blue-sensitive) aesthetic. Crafted in Cairo Montenotte, Italy. Official product page confirms 50 ISO / 120 / orthochromatic; base not stated. Sourced from filmferrania.com.

name: Mono 100
notes: Kosmo Foto Mono. 100-ISO panchromatic B&W film. Traditional B&W chemistry (not C-41 minilab compatible); can pull to ISO 50 or push to ISO 400. Rebadged emulsion from a European film producer, factory-fresh. 120 version at https://kosmofoto.com/product/kosmo-foto-mono-120-film/ . Base not officially stated.

name: Agent Shadow 400
notes: Kosmo Foto Agent Shadow. 400-ISO panchromatic B&W film for 35mm. Push-processable four stops to ISO 6400. Fine-grained at box speed, more atmospheric grain and dramatic contrast when pushed. Existing emulsion from a company with over a century of film-making experience. Base not officially stated.

name: Pancro 400
notes: BERGGER Pancro 400. Two-emulsion panchromatic B&W film (silver bromide + silver iodide, differing grain sizes) for wide exposure latitude; nominal 400 ISO, usable 160-1600. Classical grain, variable with developer. Sheets coated on 175 um PET base with undercoated anti-halation layer and anti-curling layer. Datasheet PDF: https://bergger.com/en/index.php?controller=attachment&id_attachment=4 . 135 page: https://bergger.com/en/films-sheets/bergger-pancro-400-35-mm.html ; 120 page: https://bergger.com/en/films-sheets/bergger-pancro-400-rouleau-120.html

name: Washi S
notes: Film Washi 'S' - 50 iso/18 - motion-picture sound-recording film. Polyester base 120 um; anti-halation layer placed between base and emulsion (defining feature). Panchromatic (max 620 nm; not fully red-sensitive). Fine grain, very strong contrast, industrial coating. Also offered on 30.5m bulk roll. Discontinued per Washi.

name: Washi F
notes: Film Washi 'F' - 100 iso/21 - medical fluorographic X-ray film. Polyester base 100 um, coated without anti-halation layer (high diffusion/halation effect). Orthochromatic (max 575 nm; red safelight). High grain, medium contrast, hand-coated.

name: Washi D
notes: Film Washi 'D' - 500 iso/28 - aerial surveillance/cartography film. Very thin polyester base 75 um. Panchromatic (max 690 nm; process in total darkness). Pushable to 1600; can also be processed as slide. Moderate grain, strong contrast, industrial coating. Offered in 36exp, 72exp, single-use camera, and 30.5m bulk. 135 production paused/discontinued per Washi.

name: Washi W
notes: Film Washi 'W' - 25 iso/15 - orthochromatic emulsion hand-coated on Kozo paper (28 g/m2, Moriki Paper Tosa Washi), medium transparency. Orthochromatic (max 545 nm; sensitive to blue, less green, no red; red 575 nm safelight). Datasheet gives usable range ~3/6 to 12/25 iso depending on developer/light; box max sensitivity 25 iso. Very strong texture and contrast, hand-coated.

name: Washi X
notes: Film Washi 'X' - 100 iso/21 - maskless color film (no orange mask). Per Washi's official product listing it can be processed in C-41 (negative) or E6 (slide). No dedicated datasheet PDF is published; only the product-listing text is authoritative. Base not stated. (Note: this is a 100-iso maskless color film, not a panchromatic BW stock.)

name: Washi V
notes: Film Washi 'V' - 100 iso/21 - panchromatic emulsion hand-coated on Gampi paper (41 g/m2, Awagami Factory, Japan), high transparency. Panchromatic (max 640 nm; process in full darkness). Push/pull +/-2 stops. Medium texture, soft contrast, hand-coated. Production stopped (Gampi paper supply); discontinued per Washi.

name: X Film 80
notes: CatLABS X FILM 80 MKII B&W negative film. Traditional fine-grain B&W film in the vein of Kodak Panatomic-X; very fine grain, moderate contrast, wide latitude, high silver content. IE 80 (expose at 80-100; rate 50 indoors; push to 800 in low light). Offered in 120 and 4x5/8x10 sheets. Base and spectral sensitivity not explicitly stated on official page.

name: X Film 320
notes: CatLABS X FILM 320 Pro B&W negative. EI 320 nominal (rate 200 in bright sun; push to 1600 in low light). Extended red / near-IR sensitivity to ~750 nm (use R72 IR filter at ISO 3-5 for IR effect). Exceptionally fine grain, extremely clear base. Non-DX. Offered in 35mm and 120. 35mm variant listing: https://www.catlabs.info/product/xpan320 . Base not stated.
